pr.title: More SDK labeling and splits, plus corrections
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large SDK-annotation PR that split a major portion of asm/dolphin/dolphin_sdk_1.s into named Dolphin OS assembly files, added those objects to obj_files.mk, and propagated many newly identified SDK symbols through assembly callsites plus include/functions.h and include/variables.h. The diff is mostly label/name correction rather than behavior change: EXI, OS context, error, interrupt, memory, mutex, reboot, reset, RTC, serial, font, and link routines were moved or labeled with SDK names, while BSS/data symbols such as __OSErrorTable, Packet, Alarm, __OSStartTime, __OSLastInterrupt*, and ResetFunctionQueue replaced anonymous lbl_* names. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Removed roughly 6.8k lines from the monolithic asm/dolphin/dolphin_sdk_1.s and introduced separate SDK OS assembly files including OSContext.s, OSError.s, OSExi.s, OSFont.s, OSInterrupt.s, OSLink.s, OSMemory.s, OSMutex.s, OSReboot.s, OSReset.s, OSResetSW.s, OSRtc.s, and OSSerial.s.
Updated obj_files.mk to build the new OS split objects in sequence before the remaining dolphin_sdk_1.o, and corrected the DebuggerDriver object path to asm/dolphin/OdemuExi2/DebuggerDriver.o.

Relabeled many function callsites from anonymous func_* names to SDK names, especially EXI functions used by CARD and hio code: EXIImm, EXIImmEx, EXIDma, EXISync, EXISetExiCallback, EXIProbe, EXISelect, EXIDeselect, EXILock, and EXIUnlock.

Added or propagated OS SDK names such as OSLoadContext, OSSaveContext, OSGetCurrentContext, OSSetErrorHandler, OSPanic, OSEnableInterrupts, __OSMaskInterrupts, OSGetResetCode, OSGetPhysicalMemSize, OSGetSoundMode, and OSSetSoundMode.

Renamed BSS variables from raw lbl_* names to identified SDK/global names, including __OSErrorTable, Packet, Alarm, __OSStartTime, __OSLastInterruptSrr0, __OSLastInterrupt, __OSLastInterruptTime, ResetFunctionQueue, LastState, HoldUp, and HoldDown.
Updated include/functions.h and include/variables.h so the searchable symbol list matched the assembly labels and BSS/data renames.

Corrected at least one data reference from func_80347BFC to OSOnReset and corrected baselib call labels such as HSD_OSInit using OSGetPhysicalMemSize instead of the previously labeled OSGetCurrentContext.
smart_moves: Split SDK assembly by original subsystem/file boundaries instead of continuing to grow a catch-all dolphin_sdk_1.s, making later decompilation and C porting easier to scope.
Kept build ordering explicit in obj_files.mk while extracting contiguous ranges, preserving link layout expectations for matching.
Propagated labels broadly: function labels, assembly callsites, header comment stubs, BSS globals, and data references were updated together, reducing stale func_* aliases.
Used recognizable Dolphin SDK naming families to cluster related unidentified code: OS*, __OS*, EXI*, SI*, PAD*, and SRAM/sound/progressive-mode routines.
Converted many callsite-only discoveries into reusable project-wide names, so downstream PRs can reason about CARD, hio, baselib, and OS code without re-identifying the same SDK routines.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic SDK assembly file contains identifiable contiguous original SDK units, split by subsystem and add each object to obj_files.mk before attempting C matching.
Symbol-label PRs should update every consumer: asm callsites, include/functions.h, include/variables.h, BSS labels, and data pointers.
SDK labels often unlock cross-system corrections: identifying OS/EXI routines immediately clarified CARD, hio, DebuggerDriver, and sysdolphin baselib callsites.

Be careful with previous labels that are plausible but wrong; this PR shows corrections such as OSGetCurrentContext callsites being relabeled to OSGetPhysicalMemSize where the address/function identity demanded it.
BSS/data renames are useful decomp scaffolding even before structs are typed, because names like __OSStartTime and ResetFunctionQueue document ownership and expected access patterns.
naming_conventions: Public Dolphin OS functions use OS-prefixed names such as OSReport, OSPanic, OSLoadContext, OSGetFontEncode, OSGetResetCode, OSGetSoundMode, and OSSetSoundMode.
Internal Dolphin OS functions and globals use double-underscore prefixes such as __OSMaskInterrupts, __OSSetInterruptHandler, __OSStartTime, __OSLastInterrupt, and __OSErrorTable.
EXI subsystem routines use EXI-prefixed public names such as EXIImm, EXIImmEx, EXIDma, EXISync, EXIProbe, EXISelect, EXIDeselect, EXILock, and EXIUnlock.
Anonymous lbl_* storage was replaced only where there was an identifiable SDK/global name; other nearby labels remained lbl_*.
Some globals are plain subsystem-owned names without OS prefix, such as Packet, Alarm, ResetFunctionQueue, LastState, HoldUp, and HoldDown.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly split preserved original instruction bodies while changing file boundaries and symbol labels, minimizing behavioral risk.
Callsite relabeling retained branch offsets/instructions and changed only target names, e.g. func_80345B64 to EXIImm and func_8034772C to __OSMaskInterrupts.
New split files include macros.inc and explicit .section .text address range comments, matching existing assembly-file style.
BSS label renames kept the same .skip sizes and locations, indicating semantic relabeling rather than storage layout change.
Header stubs in include/functions.h were used as a central searchable map from func_* addresses to tentative or known SDK names.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/dolphin/os/OSExi.s or OSInterrupt.s from assembly to C using these labels.
Check whether EXIIntrruptHandler, TCIntrruptHandler, and EXTIntrruptHandler spellings were later corrected or preserved for matching.
Search for remaining references to func_80345B64, func_80345F4C, func_80346D80, and func_8034772C after this PR to find missed EXI/interrupt callsites.
Investigate whether BSS globals Packet, Alarm, LastState, HoldUp, and HoldDown later received typed structs or more subsystem-specific names.
Compare obj_files.mk ordering around dolphin_sdk_1.o in later SDK split PRs to reuse the safe split pattern.
